summary: Patrick McKenzie argues that micro-ISV websites with only a handful of pages are leaving money on the table by not creating content that targets long-tail search queries. He demonstrates with his own Bingo Card Creator site that even 200 words about a niche use case like 'icebreaker bingo' can generate consistent weekly revenue through organic search traffic. He explains that content serves three purposes: capturing long-tail searches, ranking for keywords you haven't anticipated, and creating linkable resources that generate natural backlinks. He contrasts his organic search results favorably against his paid AdWords spending, showing organic traffic converting better at lower cost.
key_points: Long-tail search queries drive the majority of organic traffic, but you need actual content on your site to rank for them
Even minimal content (200 words) about a niche use case can generate consistent recurring value that compounds over time
Natural English content captures unpredictable search queries that no amount of deliberate SEO optimization can target
Content creates linkability — people share useful articles but rarely share trial download pages
Organic search traffic converted to two sales that week while $20 in AdWords spending generated zero sales
money_quotes: It makes me probably $50 a week, which is not a bad return for writing a few extra pages about elementary school bingo variants.
The rest were, in general, a veritable deluge of once-in-a-lifetime queries… the kind that natural English snaps up like hotcakes and all the SEO in the world won't get you.
Very few people are going to say 'Wow, check out this trial demo download' (if they do about your software, mazeltov, you are going to be rich like a king).
If you put up, say, a page about how to use bingo to assess reading difficulties, teachers will swap it via email, link it on their blogs, and chat about it around the water cooler.
Content also has a nice property called linkability.

Remember, serving pages is essentially free at the margin. For the 80% of folks who visit my Dolch sight word lists and leave without viewing another page on my website I pay, well, absolutely nothing.
If its not natural to do so, maybe you should be writing different content.
key_insight: Creating useful content for your target audience is the cheapest and most sustainable customer acquisition channel because it captures the long tail of search queries that paid advertising and minimal websites can never reach.
